title: Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision
summary: A decision card that prevents the dangerous mistake of treating chemical or severe contamination as an ordinary boil-water event.
content:
A decision card that prevents the dangerous mistake of treating chemical or severe contamination as an ordinary boil-water event.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
steps: Read the exact wording and contamination cause; do not replace it with memory or a social-media summary.
For do-not-drink, use verified alternative water for ingestion and follow official limits for bathing, dishes, and laundry.
For do-not-use, prevent all contact with the affected supply unless authorities name a specific permitted use.
Document which water source serves each task and update the household table whenever the authority changes the notice.
warnings: Never boil chemically contaminated water unless authorities explicitly direct it.
Do not use affected water in humidifiers, medical devices, pets, or food equipment without approval.
common_mistakes: Starting Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Do-not-drink versus do-not-use water advisory decision.']
